root_text.an5.113:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu abhabbo sammāsamādhiṁ upasampajja viharituṁ.
root_text.an5.113:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akkhamo hoti rūpānaṁ, akkhamo saddānaṁ, akkhamo gandhānaṁ, akkhamo rasānaṁ, akkhamo phoṭṭhabbānaṁ.
root_text.an5.113:1.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu abhabbo sammāsamādhiṁ upasampajja viharituṁ.
root_text.an5.113:2.1: Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu bhabbo sammāsamādhiṁ upasampajja viharituṁ.
root_text.an5.113:2.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu khamo hoti rūpānaṁ, khamo saddānaṁ, khamo gandhānaṁ, khamo rasānaṁ, khamo phoṭṭhabbānaṁ.
root_text.an5.113:2.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu bhabbo sammāsamādhiṁ upasampajja viharitun”ti.

translation_text.an5.113:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant who has five qualities can’t enter and remain in right immersion.
translation_text.an5.113:1.3: It’s when a mendicant can’t endure sights, sounds, smells, tastes, and touches.
translation_text.an5.113:1.4: A mendicant who possesses these five qualities can’t enter and remain in right immersion.
translation_text.an5.113:2.1: A mendicant who has five qualities can enter and remain in right immersion.
translation_text.an5.113:2.3: It’s when a mendicant can endure sights, sounds, smells, tastes, and touches.
translation_text.an5.113:2.4: A mendicant who possesses these five qualities can enter and remain in right immersion.”
